settings.title: Four Business Expenses People Forget to Claim
settings.excerpt: Small deductions add up over a year, and these are the ones we most often have to remind clients about.
settings.body:
Every January we review returns and find the same legitimate expenses left off. None of them are exotic. They are simply easy to overlook in a busy year.

  • A portion of home utility costs when you work from home
  • Professional subscriptions and trade memberships
  • Mileage for business journeys in your own car
  • Bank charges and interest on business accounts

Why these slip through

The reason these get missed is rarely carelessness. They are small, scattered through the year, and often paid from a personal account without a second thought. A single trade subscription or a few work journeys feels too minor to record, but added across twelve months the total is real money, and it is money you are entitled to claim.

The home costs catch people out most. If you genuinely work from home, a fair share of your heating, lighting, and broadband can usually be claimed. The same goes for the mileage clocked up visiting clients or collecting supplies in your own car, which is easy to forget once the journey is over.

A habit that makes it easy

Keep a simple record as you go and these become effortless to claim. The hard version is trying to reconstruct a whole year from memory in the final week. A note on your phone or a single spreadsheet, updated when the cost actually happens, removes nearly all of the year-end scramble.

If you are unsure whether something qualifies, ask before you file rather than after. A quick question costs nothing, and it is far easier than amending a return once it has gone in.
